summary: Optimized the local dev restart loop by aligning stop coverage with the full dev service set, starting dev targets through bounded parallel make fan-out, skipping current Go rebuilds, and skipping current Landing Web dependency installs.

acceptance_results.criterion: make stop stops the same dev service set that make dev starts.
acceptance_results.reason: The stop target now fans out to subscription-external, subscription, billing, notification, acquisition-api, cadence-engine, landing, and admin stop targets.

acceptance_results.criterion: make dev uses bounded parallelism for the local dev service set.
acceptance_results.reason: The dev target now invokes a recursive make with -j$(DEV_JOBS), defaulting to 8, over the dev service target list.

acceptance_results.criterion: Warm Go builds skip rebuilds when outputs are current.
acceptance_results.reason: The second build reported the subscription-external binary current and skipped go build.

acceptance_results.criterion: Landing and admin frontend dependency checks skip npm install when node_modules is current.
acceptance_results.reason: Landing now matches the existing admin warm dependency check using node_modules/.package-lock.json against package-lock.json.

acceptance_results.criterion: No package manifests, lockfiles, Go modules, service source files, schemas, or deploy targets are changed.
acceptance_results.reason: The only source change is the Makefile plus harness issue/work-order/handoff artifacts.

cleanup_notes: No long-running process was started by validation except a local subscription-external binary build.
The built subscription-external binary is ignored by git and was left as a normal local build artifact.
completed_work: Created TMP-062 classified defect issue and work order.
Identified root cause: make stop omitted subscription-external, billing, and cadence-engine while make dev started them, causing stale processes and port drift.
Changed make stop and stop-all to stop the full dev service set in parallel.
Changed make dev and dev-all to use bounded parallel recursive make fan-out.
Added a reusable Makefile Go binary freshness check for local dev build targets.
Changed dev-landing to skip npm install when dependencies are current.
known_gaps: A full make stop && make dev smoke was not run to avoid disrupting currently running local services and because make dev starts long-lived background processes.
Fresh checkouts without frontend node_modules still need npm install and therefore depend on npm cache or registry speed.
Existing supervisor warnings about historical ledger drift remain unrelated to TMP-062.
operator_message: TMP-062 is complete: repeated stop && make dev now avoids stale dev services, unnecessary Go rebuilds, and unnecessary Landing Web npm installs.
risk_notes: No dependency manifests, lockfiles, Go modules, service runtime code, schema, or production Docker/deploy targets changed.
Parallel dev startup can interleave console output; DEV_JOBS can be lowered when debugging startup logs.

validation.output_excerpt: assignment_decision allow; no blocking or review reasons
make --no-print-directory -j8 stop-subscription-external stop-subscription stop-billing stop-notification stop-acquisition-api stop-cadence-engine stop-landing stop-admin
make --no-print-directory -j8 dev-subscription-external dev-subscription dev-billing dev-notification dev-acquisition-api dev-cadence-engine dev-landing dev-admin
Landing Web dependencies current; skipping npm install.
Admin Panel dependencies current; skipping npm install.
Subscription External Service built successfully; Subscription External Service binary current; skipping build.
All services built successfully dry-run completed.
ok true; only existing historical/schedulable warnings
